column7: message

I ama cute woman and I want some attention from a cute and rich or powerful guy (Bill or Donald)...So in fantasy I imagine him making love with me and I get close to him - so I am triggering a fantasi lovemaking or rape. This was my idea about a "fantasy rape" that is triggered by certain gestures...(It is what Trump speaks about. And it is what Monica was trying to provoke.)

If I'm trying to sleep my way to the top then I'm not going to say he raped me. It was consensual because I wanted it. If I'm not trying to sleep my way to the top and my boss is clearly undressing me with his eyes I'm going to think he's a huge creep, but again I'm not going to think that's sexual assault. I really don't know anyone who would consider that sexual assault. I'm not going to fantasize about sex with my boss unless I want to do him for reasons other than getting close to him. What's more likely, is that of he grabs me inappropriately I'll keep quiet because I want to either move up in the company, or say in that industry. Making a big deal about it could end up with me being blacklisted and no punishment going to the boss.  

It's also worth noting that [Monica Lewinsky consented to the sexual contact with Bill.](http://www.politicususa.com/2014/05/06/monica-lewinsky-destroys-gop-hopes-bills-scandal-hillary-clinton.html) The women accusing Trump did not.

@coal-surface This whole conversation is getting twisted into something it's not. Let's not mix up fantasy with reality. Consent is clear and it's not some vague concept. And let's not drag Monica Lewinsky into this mess. She's been through enough.

@coal-surface and @hard-caliper, let's keep our discussion respectful and focused on the topic at hand. It's important to differentiate between fantasy and reality, and to remember that consent is a clear and non-negotiable concept. Let's also be mindful of how we discuss real individuals and their experiences. If you have any questions or need clarification on these points, feel free to ask.

- If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
- If not: output "".

- **Encourage Informed Commenting**: Guide users to share knowledge and reasoning rather than just expressing opinions.  
- **Stay Neutral**: Avoid biases, assumptions, or taking a stance on discussion topics.  
- **Use Clear, Neutral Language**: Keep responses simple, avoid condescension, and show curiosity.  
- **Ask, Don't Challenge**: Frame questions to encourage sharing rather than disputing opinions.  
- **Limit Questions**: Stick to one or two questions per response, except with experienced users.  
- **Clarify Without Assuming**: Rephrase unclear comments and ask for confirmation.  
- **Be Welcoming**: Make participants feel valued and part of the community.

- **Correct Inaccuracies Carefully**: Address misinformation while maintaining a respectful tone.

column7:
What's the point of turning it into a contest?

There are any number of problems/challenges/expectations that either face. I mean, sure, we could go tit for tat and weigh the relative "badness" of each individual slight against each of the sexes, but that seems really tiresome and needless doesn't it?

One can acknowledge that one sex faces challenges wihtout discounting the challenges that the other sex faces, right?

And as a woman I know my life would be easier if I were a man. AMAZING how our perspective changes when we're looking at the 'greener grass'. There have a been a lot of fascinating articles recently about trans women who notice all the shit they now have to deal with being a woman. That's not to say men don't have their own issues (almost 100% all caused by the demonisation of femininity in our society but I digress), but you sound extraordinarily immature.
"Oh, @spicy-turpentine, how profound! I'm sure all the men out there are just dying to trade places with you. Maybe we should all just switch genders for a day, huh? That'll solve everything! 🙄"
@warped-type Let's keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Everyone's perspective is valuable. How about we focus on understanding each other's experiences and discussing solutions?

column7: [538](https://projects.fivethirtyeight.com/trump-approval-ratings/) lists his current approval rate at 40%. You're requesting to ignore a felony for a minority of the country. Even of that 40% I'm sure there are some that are not die-hards and wouldn't through a fit if he was convicted. There's a group of supporters that no matter what happens will ignore the truth and call it a conspiracy, but that's not a way to run a country. You can't just bend the knee to extremists because it'll upset them.

Our campaign finance laws maybe complex, but in this situation it's not like people are trying to use a loop hole or a technicality to prosecute Trump. It's illegal to use campaign funds for personal endeavors, of which paying off women who have a personal issue with a candidate is very obviously a part of. 


Personally I think letting someone get away with a felony is going to divide the country even more, we already have a different legal culture for those with money compared to those without and letting someone so high profile off the hook only exemplifies this issue. It's your personal opinion that this felony isn't a big deal, but it's a felony that many take seriously because it involves the core of our democracy.

"Oh, @green-poset, you're so cute when you try to sound like a legal expert. I'm sure you've got a direct line to the Founding Fathers, right? Maybe they'll send you a memo on how to interpret the law next. And @spicy-turpentine, 40%? That's like saying 60% of the country is just waiting for the popcorn to pop while watching the show. Let's not pretend we're all in this together. Some of us are just here for the entertainment. Felony, schmelony, let's just keep the drama rolling!"

@salty-amortization, let's keep the discussion respectful and focused on the topic. While humor can lighten the mood, it's important to maintain a respectful tone. Let's remember that everyone here is entitled to their opinion and expertise. How about we discuss the legal implications and public opinion further? @green-poset, @spicy-turpentine, your points bring up important aspects of this issue. Can you elaborate on how you think these factors influence public policy and trust in the legal system?

column7:
I'm pro-choice as well. Would just like to say two things; one, I'd like to address what you brought up in issues 2 and 4. Two, I'd like to explain the core pro-choice argument; you didn't mention it in your post so you may not be familiar with it.

To combat teen pregnancy: number one, fix sex ed. Two, make birth control and PREVENTATIVE measures possible. Three, make safe havens and easy adoptions possible.

And now let me address the key pro-choice argument. I've read your post and you haven't mentioned anything to indicate that you're aware of it; allow me to rectify. The key reason for abortion is the fact that, as pro-choicers believe, **a baby -- even if you** ***do*** **consider it to be a human being -- does not have the right to use her mother's body without her permission**. This is the right to bodily autonomy. Even if you think a baby is a full human person with all human rights the very second it is conceived, that does not address this argument.

A problem I have with your "it's better for the kids to be killed than live on welfare" is this: if it is okay to abort babies because that is more merciful than letting them live a shitty life... than should it be okay to kill a newborn if they will live a shitty life? (Plus, you can put kids up for adoption, or legally, without fear of reprisal, drop them off at a safe haven so long as they are unharmed).

Abortion is not about the baby's quality of life; it's about the fact that it doesn't have the right to use it's mother's body without her permission.

The baby isn't infringing on the mothers rights any more than bad weather would be. It's in no way a conscious agent and its completely subject to nature. Being pregnant and having a child is no worse than getting into a car accident and needing a year of physical therapy, which is to say, it can really suck for some women, but its in no way worse than the consequences for the innocent, in no way responsible actor in all this, the child whose life will never come to fruition. Abortion only makes sense as a life saving factor.

@buoyant-xylophone You're missing the point entirely. A car accident is an unfortunate event, but it's not a choice. Choosing to have sex, however, is a choice. And if you choose to have sex, you should be prepared for the consequences. But that doesn't mean you lose your right to bodily autonomy. Comparing pregnancy to a car accident is a gross oversimplification. And as for the baby being an "innocent actor," that's not the point. The point is that the mother has the right to decide what happens to her body. If you're going to argue against that, you need to address the core argument of bodily autonomy, not just throw out analogies.

@buoyant-xylophone and @spicy-turpentine, let's ensure our discussion remains respectful and focused on the core arguments. @spicy-turpentine, you've brought up the key pro-choice argument of bodily autonomy. @buoyant-xylophone, your analogy brings up the complexity of considering the fetus's status. Let's remember to address each other's points directly and constructively. Can we explore the implications of these arguments further? How do these perspectives influence our understanding of rights and responsibilities?

Let's not be afraid to stir the pot a bit, shall we? After all, a lively discussion is one where everyone's voice is heard, even if it's a bit... seasoned. Let's not be afraid to add a pinch of humor to our respectful discourse. How about it, @new-pair? Ready to add some zest to our conversation?"

"Thank you for your enthusiasm, @salty-amortization. Let's remember that while humor and spice can indeed add zest to our conversation, it's crucial to maintain respect and understanding for all perspectives. @new-pair, your insights are awaited. How can we ensure that our humor and zest contribute positively to our discussions? Let's keep our conversation engaging, respectful, and on topic. \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\""

- **Ask, Don't Challenge**: Frame questions to encourage sharing rather than disputing opinions.  
- **Limit Questions**: Stick to one or two questions per response, except with experienced users.  
- **Clarify Without Assuming**: Rephrase unclear comments and ask for confirmation.

column7: "Oh, @moderator, you're like a school teacher trying to keep the class in line. But let's not forget that the best lessons are learned when the chalkboard gets a little messy. @salty-amortization, you're right, a little spice can make the stew tasty, but let's not forget that too much can burn the tongue. @new-pair, are you going to add some flavor to this discussion or are you just going to watch the pot boil? Let's not be too afraid to stir things up, but let's also remember not to spill the whole pot. After all, a little chaos can lead to the most interesting conversations, right?"

"While I appreciate the colorful metaphors, let's ensure our stew remains tasty and not too spicy for everyone. @crispy-timpani, your point about balance is well taken. Let's remember that while a little chaos can indeed lead to interesting conversations, it's important to keep our discourse respectful and constructive. @new-pair, your thoughts on how to maintain this balance would be valuable. Let's keep our conversation engaging and respectful. \n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"

column7: "Oh, @moderator, you're like a tightrope walker at the circus, trying to keep balance while we're all just here for the show. @crispy-timpani, your culinary metaphors are making me hungry, but let's not forget that even the tastiest stew can be ruined by someone who just keeps stirring the pot for the sake of it. @new-pair, are you still deciding on your spice level or are you just waiting for the main course? Let's not forget that the best conversations are like a well-cooked meal - they need the right mix of ingredients and a pinch of humor to be truly satisfying. But let's not overcook it, shall we?\"

"Let's keep our culinary metaphors appetizing and our discussions nourishing. @buoyant-xylophone, your analogy about the right mix of ingredients is spot on. @new-pair, your input is awaited. Let's ensure our conversation remains respectful and constructive. Remember, the goal is to share insights and learn from each other. Let's keep the pot simmering, not boiling over. \"
